signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: Trump's weekend announcement of 25% tariffs on European autos sets up immediate downside pressure for EUR/USD toward the 1.1600 handle. This trade protectionism reduces European export competitiveness and reinforces the EUR COT net short of -18,293 contracts. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines if the central bank acknowledges these new downside growth risks.

signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention Risk
signals.text: JPY's 1W momentum (+1.08%) runs against the 300bp US-JPY yield gap, but confirmed $34.5 billion in yen intervention creates a floor for JPY strength. JPY COT net short of -88,543 (30.6% long) remains heavily crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to a massive short-squeeze if US-Iran peace talks further depress the USD. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) is the catalyst for resolving the yield gap through potential policy normalization.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) creates a significant crowded-long unwind risk as the pair approaches the 0.7200 resistance level. While the RBA 4.10% rate and hike bias provide a +35bp carry advantage over the USD, retail sentiment is 90% short, suggesting a contrarian squeeze could precede any fundamental reversal. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 0% hike priced) on 5 May determines whether the hike bias remains intact to support these positions.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning Overstretch
signals.text: GBP COT net long +32,637 (67.3% long) signals an overstretched bullish bias that risks exhaustion following the 1W gain of +0.81%. The BoE active hold signals June risks, which is likely to cap upside as the 5.01% UK 10Y yield faces pressure from a bull-steepening US curve. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for confirming if the bank will pivot toward a more dovish stance.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Volatility + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: WTI crude's 2.98% Friday slide to $101.9 weighs on the Loonie, supporting USD/CAD upside despite the USD's general G8 weakness. CAD COT net short of -54,703 (23.8% long) shows heavy institutional bearishness, while 80% of retail traders are long, creating a high-volatility environment at the 1.3588 close. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines if the hold bias will shift to counter oil-driven inflation volatility.
